question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R4 is reconfigured with NET 49.0099.0000.0000.0004.00, intending to
move R4 from area 49.0001 into 49.0099 while the rest of the fabric
stays in 49.0001. In practice the move does NOT take effect: IOS-XR
permits multiple NETs per IS-IS instance for area migration, so this
ADDS 49.0099 alongside the original 49.0001 NET rather than replacing
it. Combined with `is-type level-2-only` (L2 adjacencies span areas),
no adjacency drops, no ADJCHANGE fires, and there is no data-plane or
SR-TE impact - the only artifact is a stray second NET in the
running-config. Negative/no-op test: the agent should detect the
config oddity without confabulating an outage.
expected_rca:
Effectively no operational root cause. On R4 the IS-IS NET was set to 49.0099.0000.0000.0004.00 intending to move R4 into area 49.0099, but IOS-XR permits multiple NETs per IS-IS instance and ADDED 49.0099 alongside the original 49.0001.0000.0000.0004.00 instead of replacing it.
With is-type level-2-only the L2 adjacencies span areas, so the existing L2 adjacencies remain valid and there is no data-plane impact; the only artifact is a stray second NET in the running-config.
Fix: remove the added 49.0099 NET so R4 is left with its single NET 49.0001.0000.0000.0004.00.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario. IOS-XR
permits multiple NET addresses per IS-IS instance for area-migration
use cases, so this apply ADDS a second NET rather than replacing
the first; L2 adjacencies are unaffected and no ADJCHANGE fires.
Diagnostic ground truth lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R4 `show running-config router isis LAB | include net`: TWO net lines — `49.0001.0000.0000.0004.00` (baseline) AND `49.0099.0000.0000.0004.00` (added by apply). XR accepts up to 3 NETs.
Every other router `show running-config router isis LAB | include net`: exactly one net line in area 49.0001.
R3 `show isis database` (R4's LSPID): R4 now advertises area-id 49.0099 in addition to 49.0001 — visible in the Area Addresses TLV.
Note: revert in this manifest re-adds the original NET but does NOT explicitly `no net 49.0099...`; the baseline restoration is a `no net` step the operator must do separately if cleanup is required.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
 net 49.0099.0000.0000.0004.00

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
 net 49.0001.0000.0000.0004.00
